synthetic_75_clinician.body: Yes, establishing care with a primary care physician is a good idea. These visits are for preventive care—screening for things like high blood pressure, updating vaccinations, and addressing age-appropriate screenings like the Pap smear you mentioned.

I was raised the exact same way and was so confused when I first had to navigate healthcare on my own. It felt weird to make an appointment when I wasn't sick. But now I go for a physical every year and it gives me a lot of peace of mind. You're definitely not alone in feeling this way!

It's a great question. Establishing care with a primary care physician (PCP) is really valuable. It's also the time to make sure you're up to date on vaccines and screenings.
